steps.title: Set up the ratio
steps.content: The ratio of $j$ to $k$ is given as 11 to 12, so $$j/k = \frac{11}{12}$$.

steps.title: Apply the same factor to both terms
steps.content: Multiplying $j$ by 17 gives $17j$. To keep the ratio unchanged, we must multiply $k$ by the same factor: $$(17j)/(17k) = j/k = \frac{11}{12}$$. Thus $k$ is also multiplied by 17. The answer is 17.
